source.title: Safwa-tul-Masaadir (صفوة المصادر) — Arabic-English edition
source.author: Maulana Mushtaaq Ahmed Charthawali
source.section: Al-Thulathi al-Mujarrad (الثلاثي المجرد) — the three root-letter verbs

fields.masdar: verbal noun (المصدر), as printed with definite article
fields.bab: one of: nasara, daraba, samia, fataha, karuma, hasiba
fields.verb_type: book subsection: sahih, mudaaf, mahmuz_fa/ain/lam, mithal_wawi/yai, ajwaf_wawi/yai, naqis_wawi/yai, lafif_mafruq/maqrun
fields.book_page: page number in the printed book
fields.transcription_note: present only where the print showed something unusual

entries.meaning: to be fixed, to be established

entries.transcription_note: masdar printed with bare alif (no fatha on ال)
masdar printed with bare alif (no fatha on ال)
ism fail printed as سَعِيْدٌ (sifah form, not فَاعِلٌ pattern)

entries.meaning: to be angry to be displeased

entries.transcription_note: amr and nahi printed with final damma (sic; paradigm expects sukun اِلْبَثْ / لَا تَلْبَثْ); verified at high zoom against sukun glyphs elsewhere

entries.meaning: to catch up, to be attached
to come to nothing, to die

entries.transcription_note: masdar printed without shadda on ط (verified at 5x zoom), unlike الطَّحْنُ/الطَّرْحُ in adjacent rows

entries.meaning: to be bad, to be wicked
to be pious, to be in order

entries.transcription_note: masdar printed with sukun on ظ (اَلْعَظْمَةُ, verified at 5x zoom), not the common عَظَمَة

entries.meaning: to be bad, to be ugly
to be generous, to be respected
to bite, to grab with the teeth
to be wise, to be proficient

entries.transcription_note: masdar hamza vowel small in print; read as fatha (اَلسَّأَمُ)

entries.meaning: to cause loss to some one (financially)

entries.transcription_note: hamza seat after طْ read as line hamza, matching اَلْبُرْءُ pattern

entries.meaning: to be easy, to be light

entries.transcription_note: no fatha printed on the alif of النَّوْمُ in this cell (other cells print اَلْ)
listed under ajwaf yai but printed mudari is يَغُوْطُ with waw; masdar printed اَلْغَيْطُ with ya

entries.meaning: to become clear, to be separated

entries.transcription_note: masdar shadda vowel on ت ambiguous at print size; transcribed as kasra (اَلتِّيْهُ)
masdar final letter/vowel very small in print; transcribed اَلْعَمٰى
masdar final letter/vowel very small in print; transcribed اَلْوَجٰى

entries.meaning: to be incapable, to be exhausted
entries.transcription_note: mudari printed as يَعْىٰ (three letters, dagger alif over dotless final ya, no medial ya tooth)
